name: Alliance Youth Services dba The Pointe

findings.rule_description: Provider shall protect clients from abuse, prevent abuse
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff member exposing the clients to potential harm or acts of violence during the road rage incident. The staff members  actions allowed the other driver and clients to engage in a physical altercation resulting in acts of violence and potential harm. During the investigation inspection, client interviews substantiated that the staff member did not take any action to protect the clients.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report alleged neglect and criminal activity to law enforcement or Child Protective Services. During the inspection, through documentation review, it was observed that neither staff involved in the incident notified Child Protective Services or law enforcement.

name: Care Youth Corporation - Falcon Ridge Ranch dba Rite Of Passage Inc/Falcon Ridge Ranch

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance by not maintaining a staff-to-client ratio of one staff to every four clients. During an investigation, provider documentation verified that on Sunday, November 10, 2024 the provider was not compliant with the required staff to student ratio for multiple hours that day.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing supervision commensurate with the behaviors of each client. During an inspection, multiple interviews disclosed and facility video coverage recorded a client with a history of impulsive behaviors was not adequately supervised while in possession of hazardous chemicals. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting a critical incident to the Office of Licensing within one business day. During a complaint investigation inspection, the complaint being reviewed was identified as a critical incident that was not reported to the Office of Licensing by the provider. This is a repeat citation.

findings.rule_description: Provider shall protect clients from abuse, prevent abuse
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by compromising the health and safety of clients and failing to protect clients from harm. During the investigation inspection, a staff member provided a client with chemicals to clean a water bottle and two additional staff failed to supervise clients using cleaning chemicals. A client intentionally added chemicals to another client’s water bottle with the intent to harm them. A third client drank from the water bottle and reported their stomach hurt. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not immediately reporting allegations of child abuse to Child Protective Services intake or law enforcement. During a complaint investigation inspection, the information gathered supported that an alleged child abuse incident occurred on 11/10/2024 and the provider reported the allegations to Child Protective Services on 11/15/2024.

findings.rule_description: Submitting application for an initial background screening
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by allowing a staff member without a conditional or eligible background clearance to work without being directly supervised. During an investigation, facility video coverage recorded a staff member who required direct supervision and was not being directly supervised.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not having current Provider Code of Conduct or documentation of training for staff response to client leaving program without permission in staff files within 30 days of hire. During staff file review the licensor observed that the Provider Code of Conduct was not the current form and there was no documentation of training for staff response to client leaving the program without permission.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not maintaining the required staff-to-client one to four client ratio. During the inspection the information gathered from interviews and provider documentation substantiated that staff did not provide clients with required staff to client ratio mandated for client safety during an outing on 10/26/2024. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting private placements to the Office by the fifth business day of the month for August and September 2024. During the inspection the licensor verified through QuickSight reporting that the provider had not reported private placements to the office by the fifth business day of the month for August and September 2024. This is a repeat non compliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not maintaining required staff ratios. During the inspection the provider was observed to be out of staff to client ratio on 1 group staffed at 1 staff  to 8 clients. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a critical incident by the next business day.  During review of critical incidents it was identified that the incident occurred on 3-22-24, was not reported until 3-26-24. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to maintain a staff-to-client ratio of one staff to every four clients. The information gathered throughout the investigation substantiated that the provider was out of staff to student ratio throughout multiple shifts between 02/09/2024 and 02/22/2024.

findings.rule_description: Maintain 1 staff to every 4 client ratio
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not having enough staff to meet the required 1:4 ratio. During the inspection it was observed that the provider was out of ratio on 12/17/2023 and 12/18/2023. This is a repeat noncompliance.

findings.rule_description: Maintain 1 staff to every 4 client ratio
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to maintain a 1 to 4 staff to student ratio and supervision. During an on-site inspection, facility video coverage and provider documentation was reviewed and multiple interviews were conducted. The evidence substantiated that the provider was out of compliance with staff to student ratio and supervision on the four shifts reviewed.

name: Care Youth Corporation dba Rite Of Passage Inc/Lava Heights Academy
Catalyst Residential Treatment Center dba Gray House

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with R501-1-8(1)(i) by not having prescription medication stored in its original pharmacy packaging. During the inspection, loose pills were found in clients’ medication folders and pill bottles’ labels had been removed. Technical assistance was previously provided for this rule on 10/21/2025. This rule noncompliance is a repeat noncompliance as noted on 12/28/2023, 02/29/2024, 03/25/2024, 05/30/2024, 06/25/2024, and 08/12/2024.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff member not treating a client with dignity and being disrespectful during an incident on 02/28/2024 by insulting the client numerous times.  This non-compliance was confirmed during an inspection on 04/01/2024. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with the rule by not having medication in original pharmacy packaging, and by not having the facility maintained and free of health and fire hazards. During the inspection on 2/29/2024, the licensor observed that prescription medication was not consistently being stored with its original packaging.

This rule non-compliance is a repeat non-compliance. This rule non-compliance is a repeated non-compliance from 2/29/2024, as this non-compliance item was not resolved.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not having all annual trainings completed and documented for employees. During the inspection on 2/29/2024, the licensor observed that the provider did not have documentation of multiple annual trainings, and had not done some annual trainings.

This rule non-compliance is a repeat non-compliance. This rule non-compliance is a repeated non-compliance from 2/29/2024, as this non-compliance item was not resolved.

The provider was out of compliance with the rule by not consistently reporting private placements to the Office of Licensing. At the time of the inspection on 2/29/2024, multiple months of private placement reporting were absent.

This rule non-compliance is a repeat non-compliance. This rule non-compliance is a repeated non-compliance from 2/29/2024, as this non-compliance item was not resolved.

The provider was out of compliance with the rule by not reporting critical incidents to the office of licensing. During the inspection on 2/29/2024, the licensor discussed various incidents with the provider that were critical incidents that had not been reported.

This rule non-compliance is a repeat non-compliance. This rule non-compliance is a repeated non-compliance from 2/29/2024, as this non-compliance item was not resolved.

The provider was out of compliance with the rule by not having medication in original pharmacy packaging, and by not having the facility maintained and free of health and fire hazards. During the inspection, the licensor observed that prescription medication was not consistently being stored with its original packaging.

This rule non-compliance is a repeat non-compliance.

The provider was out of compliance with the rule by not reporting critical incidents to the office of licensing. During the inspection, the licensor discussed various incidents with the provider that were critical incidents that had not been reported.

This rule non-compliance is a repeat non-compliance.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report 2 separate critical incidents to Licensing.  On 08/30/2023, the investigator and licensor discovered during client and staff interviews that a critical incident occurred on 08/26/2023 and 08/28/2023.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report 3 separate critical incidents to Licensing.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not submitting a critical incident report within one business day.  The incident was discovered by the provider on 07/12/2023 and was reported to Licensing on 07/14/2023.

findings.rule_description: Staff assigned for direct supervision shall not be counted at the same time in the ratio
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not having not staff directly supervising a client on precautions.

name: Catalyst Residential Treatment Center dba Red House
Cinnamon Hills Youth Crisis Center
address: 770 E St George Blvd, Saint George, UT, 84770

findings.rule_description: Medication and hazardous items maintenance and storage
findings.finding_text: The Licensee was out of compliance with rule R501-1-8(3) by not maintaining hazardous items with consideration of the safety and risk level of the population served. During the inspection, chemicals were not in locked cabinets when not in use.	This is a repeat noncompliance as noted on 1/12/24, 7/8/25, 10/2/25. Technical assistance was provided on 9/2/25

The Licensee was out of compliance with R380-600-7(16)(b) by not notifying the legal guardian of each involved client within a 24-hour period from the time of the incident. During the client file inspection clients parents and or guardians had not been notified of incidents within 24 hours. This is a repeat noncompliance as noted on 1/30/25.

The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(b) by a staff member not ensuring that a client was treated with dignity and respect. During an investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a staff member yelled at and used profanity directed at a client. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 05/11/2023, 11/16/2023, and 02/07/2025.

findings.rule_description: Provider shall protect clients from abuse, prevent abuse
findings.finding_text: The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-5(4) by a staff member acting in a way that harmed and compromised the health and safety of multiple clients. During an investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a staff member provided and used electronic vaping devices with multiple clients, allowed a client to view sexually inappropriate photos of a minor, video recorded multiple clients engaged in physical altercations and did not intervene, and allowed clients to record themselves engaging in simulated lewd acts and other licensee prohibited actions on that staff’s cell phone. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 12/26/2023 and 02/07/2024.

The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(b) by a staff member not ensuring a client’s right to dignity and respect. During an investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a staff member yelled at and told a client to shut up. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 05/09/2023, 11/16/2023, and 02/07/2025.

The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(c) by not ensuring a client’s right to be free from harm or acts of violence. During the investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a client was physically harmed and injured by two clients. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 03/19/2024, 01/30/2025, 01/30/2025, 02/07/2025, and 02/26/2025.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-5(10) by not ensuring services and supervision that is commensurate with the behaviors and needs or each client. During the investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a staff did not provide services and supervision proportionate with clients behaviors and needs. During this time, a client was physically harmed and injured by two other clients. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 04/05/2023 and 02/07/2025.

The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(c) by a staff member not ensuring a client was free from potential harm or acts of violence. During an investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a staff attempted to physically confront a client and threw a flip flop at a client’s head. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 03/19/2024, 01/30/2025, 01/30/2025, and 02/07/2025.

The licensee was out of compliance with R501-1-4(5)(b) by a staff member not using behavior management techniques that emphasize de-escalation and promote self-control. During an investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a staff member engaged in confrontational behavior with a client rather than using de-escalation techniques. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 02/07/2025.

inspections.inspection_types: Complaint, Investigation Inspection, Non On-Site Inspection

findings.finding_text: The licensee was out of compliance with R501-1-4(5)(b) by a staff member not using behavior management techniques that emphasized de-escalation and promoted self-control. During the investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a staff member’s negative conduct exacerbated an incident that other staff members were working to de-escalate.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-5(10) by a staff member not providing supervision with the skills, behaviors, and needs of each client. During the investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a staff member was focused on a cell phone while two clients engaged in a confrontation that escalated to a physical assault. This is a repeat noncompliance as previously noted on 04/05/2023, 08/16/2023, and 12/26/2023.

The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(b) by a staff member not ensuring a client had the right to be treated with dignity and respect and to be free from potential harm or acts of violence. During the inspection, the information gathered substantiated a staff member engaged in a verbal and physical confrontation with a client. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 05/09/2023, 11/16/2023, 03/19/2024, 01/30/2025, and 01/30/2025.

The licensee was out of compliance with R380-600-7(6) by withholding information that affected the office’s information gathering process. During the investigation, a review of an incident report submitted by the licensee was conducted. It was observed that the licensee omitted information pertinent to the context of the incident. This is a repeat noncompliance as previously noted on 01/30/2025.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by staff members using a physical restraint as a convenience to staff. During the investigation inspection, facility video coverage and multiple interviews substantiated that a client who was not an immediate danger to themselves or others was placed in an unnecessary physical restraint. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by violating the client’s right to be free from potential harm and acts of violence. During the investigation inspection, the information gathered substantiated that a staff member acted outside the scope of their training, and was physically aggressive with a client while conducting a physical restraint. This is a repeat citation.

The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(c) by a staff member not ensuring a client’s right to be free from potential harm and acts of violence. During the investigation inspection, the information gathered substantiated that a staff member acted outside the scope of their training, and was physically aggressive with a client while conducting a physical restraint.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff member initiating a physical restraint that was not used as a temporary means of physical containment to protect the consumer, other persons, or property from immediate harm. During the inspection, facility video coverage and administrative/staff interviews substantiated that a staff member acted outside the scope of their training and initiated an unnecessary restraint on a client who was being uncooperative but was not an immediate danger to themself or others. This is a repeat citation.

name: Copper Hills Youth Center
address: 5899 W Rivendell Dr, West Jordan, UT, 84081

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting a critical incident to the Office within one business day. During review of critical incidents it was identified that the incident occurred on 11/18/2024 and was not reported until 11/20/2024.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to provide supervision commensurate with the behaviors and needs of a client. Throughout the investigation, multiple interviews, provider records, and additional documents were reviewed. The information gathered substantiated that a client who had expressed suicidal ideation and intent was not provided with the level of supervision needed to keep them safe.

findings.rule_description: Provider shall protect clients from abuse, prevent abuse
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by compromising the health and safety of a client. Throughout the investigation, multiple interviews, provider records and additional documents were reviewed, and professional evaluations were considered. The information gathered substantiated that a client who had expressed suicidal ideation and a plan was not provided with the care required to keep them safe.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not following their suicide prevention policy. Throughout an investigation, multiple interviews, documentation, and provider records substantiated that the provider did not follow their suicide prevention policy.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting a critical incident to the Office of Licensing within one business day and not reporting a critical incident to the legal guardian of a client involved in an incident within a 24-hour period from the time of the incident. During an investigation inspection, provider documentation and provider/legal guardian communication substantiated that a physical restraint was conducted on a client on 04/23/2024 and was not reported to the Office of Licensing or the legal guardian as required by Utah Administrative Rule. This is a repeat noncompliance citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting the critical incident within one business day. During the inspection, a critical incident that happened on 07/07/2024 was not reported until 7/15/2024. This is a repeat noncompliance.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not submitting the critical incident report within one business day. The incident occurred on 10/23/2023 and was reported on 10/30/2023.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not submitting the critical incident report within one business day. Incident occurred on 10/22/2023 and was reported on 10/24/2023.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing supervision based of the clients behavior. During the inspection a client with a history of sexual misconduct was able to sexually act with a peer. This is a repeat noncompliance from 12/6/2025.

The licensee was out of compliance with R501-19-3(3)(d)(ii) by a staff member failing to remain awake while on duty. During an investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a client and staff members observed a staff member asleep when they were assigned to supervise clients. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 12/28/2022, 05/16/2023, 09/18/2023, 12/31/2024, and 12/31/2024.

The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(c) by clients not being free from potential harm. During the investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a staff member fell asleep with an open bottle of pills that spilled in their lap and on the floor. A client observed this incident and the open bottle of pills.

The Licensee was out of compliance with R501-19-3(3)(c) for not maintaining a staff-to-client ratio of one staff to every four clients. During an investigation, the information substantiated that on 12/31/2025 the ratio was 5 staff with 22 clients for four hours that day. This is a repeat noncompliance as noted on 12/28/2022, 05/16/2023, and 09/18/2024.

The Licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(b) for a staff member that did not treat a client with dignity or respect. During the investigation, multiple interviews substantiated that a staff member told multiple clients to shut up and called them liars and attention seekers.

The licensee was out of compliance with R501-1-8(1)(a) by not ensuring the buildings and grounds are clean and maintained and free from health hazards. During an investigation inspection on 12/31/2024, a licensing investigator and licensor observed multiple environmental safety concerns throughout the client cabins.  This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 07/28/2023 and 07/25/2024.

findings.rule_description: Medication and hazardous items maintenance and storage
findings.finding_text: The licensee was out of compliance with R501-1-8(3) for not maintaining potentially hazardous items responsibly and with consideration of the safety and risk level of the population serviced. During an investigation inspection on 12/31/2024, multiple bottles of alcohol-based perfume were accessible to clients.

The licensee was out of compliance with R501-19-3(3)(f) by not performing direct supervision with line of sight check-ins every 15 minutes. During the investigation, the information gathered substantiated that a client was left unsupervised for approximately three hours. This is a repeat noncompliance noted on 05/16/2023 and 09/18/2024.

The provider was out of compliance with R501-1-10(2)(a) for not ensuring that meals are not used as incentive or punishment. During the investigation, the information gathered substantiated that meals had been used as an incentive/punishment.

The licensee was out of compliance with R380-600-7(16)(a) by not reporting a critical incident to the Office of Licensing within one business day. During an investigation, the information gathered substantiated that the licensee did not report a critical incident that occurred on 12/28/2024. This is a repeat noncompliance noted on 05/16/2023, 12/06/2023, 12/19/2023, 03/04/2024, and 04/03/2024.

findings.rule_description: Provider shall protect clients from abuse, prevent abuse
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with R380-80-5(4) by not protecting clients from harm and any action that may compromise the health and safety of clients. During the investigation, the information gathered substantiated that two clients accessed a sharp object from the staff office and used it to injure themselves.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not physically checking in with a client every 15 minutes. During the inspection a client was allowed to take a break and was not checked on for a period greater than 15 minutes. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting a critical incident. During the inspection a reportable critical incident was disclosed that was not reported to the Office of Licensing. This a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not having required posters hung for visitors, staff or clients to view. During the inspection the licensor saw that required posters were not hung on on the wall.

The Provider is out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a critical incident within one business day.  During the review of the incident it was identified that the incident occurred on 2/23/2024 and the provider gave an explanation as to the reason for the reporting being late.

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by direct care staff members failing to perform direct supervision line of sight check-ins every 15 minutes. Throughout the investigation, information was gathered through interviews and provider documentation. The evidence substantiated that clients were left unsupervised for longer than 15 minutes and engaged in unsafe behaviors. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to treat clients with dignity and respect.  During an incident around 10/24/2024, a staff member made an inappropriate comment to a client making them feel unsafe.  This was a repeat noncompliance.

findings.rule_description: Provider shall cooperate on any investigations
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not cooperating with an investigation. During the attempted inspection, provider administration told Licensing staff they would need to bring justification as to why the department needs to speak with clients. The investigators requested to interview clients and the provider denied the request.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to treat clients with dignity and respect.  During an incident on 09/26/2024, a staff member engaged in an inappropriate sexual conversation with multiple clients.  This was a repeat noncompliance.

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a physical restraint incident to the Office of Licensing within one business day. During an inspection, it was observed that a physical restraint incident occurred on 01/26/2024 and was submitted by the provider to the Office of Licensing on 02/01/2024. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report multiple allegations of verbal and physical abuse to the applicable investigative agencies. During the investigation inspection, interviews disclosed allegations of verbal and physical abuse by staff members. Some provider staff were aware of these allegations and conducted an internal investigation and did not report the allegations to the applicable investigative agencies.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule on 3 separate occasions by:

1. Failing to defer to the least restrictive method of behavior management available to control a situation and using behavior management techniques that were not trauma-informed and appropriate for the client’s behavior. During the investigation inspection, multiple interviews disclosed  that 2 staff members conducted physical restraints on 2 clients that were not a danger to themselves or others. It was disclosed that the physical restraints were unnecessary, overly aggressive, and caused the clients undue physical discomfort and pain.
2. Staff members using an unnecessary physical restraint that caused the client undue physical discomfort and pain. During the investigation inspection, interviews and provider documentation substantiated that a client was placed in an unnecessary restraint for choosing to sleep on the floor.
3. Staff members using an unnecessary restraint on a client that caused a client undue physical discomfort and pain. During the investigation inspection, interviews substantiated that some staff members consistently use pain compliance techniques when conducting physical restraints on clients that are not an immediate harm to themselves or others.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not supervising staff with an unapproved background check. During the inspection the licensor observed that a staff member requiring supervision was working with clients unsupervised.

findings.rule_description: Submitting application for an initial background screening
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not associating an employee within 2 weeks. During the inspection background checks were reviewed and a staff was associated outside the time frame.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by having a non-functioning toilet and ventilation fan in room three. At the facility, during the walkthrough the toile and ventilation fan in room three did not work. The is a repeat non-compliance.

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to comply with Section 26B-2-123.  During the investigation inspection multiple interviews substantiated that staff members conducted an unnecessary physical restraint on a client that was not in imminent harm to self others or property.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not submitting a new application for background check prior to staff working on site. During the inspection a staff on the staff list did not have a new background check application submitted as requested by the Department of Licensing and Background Screening.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not maintaining plumbing and electrical in operating order and in clean and safe condition. At the facility, during the walkthrough two toilet seats in the basement bathroom toilets were missing, multiple toilets were missing the tank covers in the facility, outlets and light switches were missing their covers, there was exposed wires on a thermostat and mold in one shower.

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by neglecting to call Child Protective Services when two clients were sexually acting out. During an onsite inspection this licensor inquired about the status of CPS involvement with administration and discovered that the investigative agency had not been called as the program had done their own investigation into the matter.

name: Pine Ridge Behavioral Health dba Behavioral Health

checklists.contact_person: Licensor(s) Conducting this Inspection: OL Staff Observing Inspection:

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing required supervision of staff who don't have a cleared background check. During the inspection on 10/1/24, the licensor observed that the provider wasn't providing required direct supervision of staff who's background checks required supervision.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not maintaining electrical systems, building and grounds. During the inspection on 8/15/24, the licensor observed broken light switches, a hole in the wall, broken door, broken window.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not maintaining the electrical systems. During the inspection on 5/10/24, the licensor observed broken light switches, electrical face plates, missing junction box covers, broken lights in clients bedrooms.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not having mechanical and electrical furnishings in working order. The building was not free from health and fire hazards. During the inspection on 2/28/24, the licensor observed that the client restrooms didn't have working vent fans. There were non-functional lights in the cafeteria, hallway and school. There was missing light covers in the school building. There was a missing electrical outlet cover in the classroom. There was a broken bracket securing a pipe to the wall. Fire extinguishers were not serviced and expired. There was exposed wires in a client bathroom.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a critical incident to the Office within one business day. During an off-site inspection, the Office reviewed an incident that occurred on 09/20/2023 and was submitted to the Office by the provider on 09/22/2023.

findings.rule_description: Direct supervision only by direct care staff
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to actively supervise clients with line-of-sight, 15-minute check-ins. During an on-site inspection, facility video coverage documented two staff that were responsible for supervising clients during nighttime hours. The two staff did not conduct line-of-sight  supervision checks as required by rule.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a direct care staff acting in a way that compromised the client's health and safety. During on and off-site inspections, the investigators conducted multiple staff interviews that disclosed a staff failed to conduct required checks on their assigned clients. During this time, a client ran from the facility at 9:30pm and his absence was not discovered until the following morning at approximately 5:00am.

The provider was out of compliance by a direct care staff filling out false information on a client supervision document. During an off-site inspection, the investigator reviewed a client supervision log filled out by a staff, on 06/26/2023 through 06/27/2023, that documented supervision checks of clients were conducted every 15-minutes. Facility video coverage and staff interviews support noncompliance with this rule.

The provider was out of compliance by two direct care staff recording false information on client supervision documents. During an on-site inspection, the investigators reviewed facility video coverage that documented two staff who did not conduct active, line-of-sight, 15-minute client supervision checks and filled out client supervision logs indicating the client supervision checks were done.

findings.rule_description: Direct supervision only by direct care staff
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to actively supervise a client with line-of-sight, 15-minute check-ins. During an inspection, multiple staff interviews supported that a staff failed to supervise clients as required by rule. This resulted in a client that left the treatment facility at approximately 9:30pm without staff awareness and the client’s absence was not discovered until the following morning at approximately 5:00am.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting a critical incident within one business day. During the inspection a critical incident happened on 9/4/2024 and was not reported to the Office of Licensing until 9/13/2024. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting a critical incident to the Office within one business day. During review of critical incidents it was identified that the incident occurred on 07/11/2024 and was not reported until 07/15/2024.
This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by providing supervision to staff requiring supervision. During the inspection on 7/9/24, the licensor observed staff members who had background checks "in process" weren't being supervised.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not following their policy of using physical restraint to protect the consumer from immediate harm. During the inspection a client was placed into a restraint that was not an immediate threat to self, others, or property.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a critical incident by the next business day. The incident occurred on 3/27/2024 and was reported on 4/1/2024. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a critical incident by the next business day. The incident occurred on 3/27/2024 and was reported on 4/1/2024. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a critical incident by the next business day.  The incident occurred on 2/22/2024 and was reported on 2/27/2024.
This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting a critical incident within one business day.  The incident occurred on 12/29/2023 and it was reported on 01/04/2024.  This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by mistreating and compromising the health and safety of a client. During an on-site inspection, licensors conducted interviews and observed facility video coverage of a physical restraint incident. The evidence substantiated that a staff member bit a client during a physical restraint incident in response to the client aggressively engaging with the staff; the client incurred an injury as a result.

findings.rule_description: Submitting application for an initial background screening
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by allowing a person with a denied background screening to work with clients. During on and off-site inspections, the evidence substantiated that a staff member’s background screening was denied on 08/31/2023. Provider documentation, multiple interviews, and facility video coverage verified that the staff member with the failed background screening worked on 10/06/2023.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff physically mistreating a client. During an on-site inspection, licensors conducted interviews and observed facility video coverage. The evidence substantiated that a staff member compromised the health and safety of a client by holding the client’s head and neck down towards their chest during a restraint. This is not an authorized technique and compromised the client’s breathing. During this restraint, facility video coverage documented the staff aggressively rubbing their hand in the client’s face.

name: Rite Of Passage dba Willard Peak Residential Treatment
Solacium Sunrise, LLC dba Sunrise RTC - The Fields

findings.finding_text: The Licensee was out of compliance with R501-1-8 (1)(a)(b) by not maintaining cleanliness of the building  and furnishings are maintained in operating order and in a clean and safe condition,. During the walkthrough inspection there is a cleanliness issue in client bedrooms, broken furniture in client living areas. This is a repeat non-compliance as noted on 12/05/2024 and 02/25/2024.

The Licensee was out of compliance with R380-600-7(16)(a-d) by not reporting a critical incident to the Office within one business day. During review of critical incidents it was identified that the incident occurred on 04/06/2025 and was not reported until 04/08/2025. 
This was a repeat noncompliance as noted on 01/05/2023, 10/10/2023, & 11/12/2024.

findings.rule_description: Provider shall protect clients from abuse, prevent abuse
findings.finding_text: The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-5(4) by staff members acting in a way that compromised the health and safety of a client. During the investigation, staff failed to secure a pair of scissors that were not being used. A client with a history of self-harm used the scissors to self-harm which resulted in medical care beyond basic first aid. This is a repeat noncompliance previously noted on 07/25/2023.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not ensuring a client was free from potential harm. During the investigation, the Licensee documentation and multiple interviews substantiated that a client gained access to an unlocked medical room and ingested medication that was not prescribed to them.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by staff not providing supervision commensurate with a client’s needs and behaviors. During an investigation inspection, the information gathered substantiated that a client who was on a higher level of supervision, obtained a staff cell phone, and used it inappropriately while unsupervised.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by supervising staff who required direct supervision. During the inspection on 12/5/24, the licensor observed staff member requiring direct supervision was not being supervised.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not performing direct supervision with line of sight check-ins every 15 minutes. During an investigation inspection, the information gathered from multiple interviews and provider documentation substantiated that a client was not provided with 15-minute, line-of-sight supervision. During this time, the staff were unaware a client left the treatment program. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not ensuring that each client is free from potential harm. During the investigation inspection, information gathered from multiple interviews and provider documentation substantiated that a client was able to leave the facility without staff knowledge and engaged in self-harm during this time. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by two staff members not performing direct supervision with line-of-sight check-ins every 15 minutes. During an investigation inspection, licensing observed two staff members 15-minute check documents at 4:42pm and again at 5:47pm. The staff had started their shifts at 3pm and had no documentation that 15-minute checks were completed. Interviews supported that staff do not consistently conduct 15-minute line-of-sight checks as required. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by staff failing to perform a 15 minute checks. In the facility, during the evening of 05/09/2024 required 15 minutes checks were not completed and a client engaged in self harming behaviors.  This was a repeat noncompliance.

checklists.contact_person: Angie Michaelson, Herbert Kendall, Jane Peterson

findings.rule_description: Direct supervision only by direct care staff
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by staff failing to actively supervise with line-of-sight supervision no less frequent than every 15 minutes. During an investigation inspection, it was observed that the staff completed a check in on clients once within a 1.5 hour period.

checklists.contact_person: Licensor(s) Conducting this Inspection: OL Staff Observing Inspection:

name: Sorenson’s Ranch School/Sorenson’s Ranch School Foundation

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to provide supervision commensurate with the skills, abilities, behaviors, and needs of each client. During the on-site inspection, multiple interviews disclosed that a staff member who was providing direct supervision for three clients, fell asleep for an undetermined period of time.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff member neglecting to provide necessary supervision to keep the client(s) free from potential harm. During an inspection, multiple interviews disclosed that the staff member was not providing active supervision when two clients engaged in inappropriate sexual misconduct.

name: Telos Residential Treatment Center, LLC dba Telos RTC

Licensor(s) Conducting this Inspection: OL Staff Observing Inspection:

name: Therapy Associates DBA Star Guides dba Star Guides
address: 377 E Riverside Dr, Saint George, UT, 84790

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance for failing to report a client rights critical incident to the Office of Licensing. During an on-site inspection, multiple interviews disclosed that one staff yelled at, swore at, and physically mistreated a client and a second staff physically mistreated a client. The staff involved failed to report this as required by rule.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by mistreating a client, by compromising the health and safety of a client, and by failing to deter others from acting. During an on-site investigation inspection, multiple interviews disclosed that a staff yelled, swore at, pulled and shoved a client up a hill that needed a break during a hike. Other staff present failed to intervene and prevent the staff from pulling and shoving the client and an additional staff eventually assisted the first staff in pulling and shoving the client up the hill.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by staff using physical restraint as a convenience for staff. During an on-site investigation inspection, multiple interviews disclosed that two staff members physically restricted a client’s freedom of movement and normal access to their body. During this incident, a client was sitting and resting during a hike. The client was not a harm or danger to themselves or others. The client had a backpack secured to their body. The staff grabbed the client by their backpack and left shoulder, pulled the client to a standing position, and used the backpack to shove the client up a hill. When the client sat down to rest this happened a second time. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report an allegation of physical child abuse by a staff member to Child Protective Services (CPS). During on and off-site inspections, multiple interviews disclosed that a staff member was alleged to have physically mistreated a client. The provider conducted their own investigation and did not report the allegation to Child Protective Services.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by conducting an unnecessary physical restraint and using unjustified pain compliance on a client. During an on-site follow-up inspection, multiple client and staff interviews disclosed that staff failed to defer to the least restrictive method of behavior management available to control a situation. The evidence substantiated that two staff conducted a physical restraint on a client that was not an immediate harm to self or others. One of the two staff involved hyperextended the client’s joint and caused the client undue physical discomfort and pain. This is a repeat citation

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff physically harming a client. During an onsite follow-up inspection, multiple interview disclosures substantiated that pain compliance was used on a client that was not a harm or danger to themselves or others. A staff member used an unjustified pain compliance technique, and hyperextended a client’s wrist, which caused the client pain. This is a repeat citation.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report an allegation of client rights noncompliance by a staff member to the Office of Licensing. During on and off-site inspections, multiple interviews disclosed that a staff member was alleged to have physically mistreated a client. The provider conducted their own investigation and did not report the allegation to the Office of Licensing.

checklists.contact_person: Licensor(s) Conducting this Inspection: McCabe Peterson OL Staff Observing Inspection:

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing adequate equipment or clothing for the environment. During the inspection it was learned that a client had soiled their day clothes and for two days hiked in sweatpants. It was also learned that clients are not provided more toilet paper when they run out before the next drop.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not conducting foot checks. During the inspection interviewed revealed that staff would check clients feet from a distance of 10-20 feet. These checks were done to ensure that client's had cleaned their feet.

findings.rule_description: Body cooling available for temperatures over 80 degrees
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing water to cool the body. During the inspection staff and clients revealed that water is rarely used to cool the body as it is a "waste of water." Only when the clients are at a water source would staff consider allowing this to happen.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by having a staff not trained in CPR or first aid. During the inspection staff files were reviewed and there was no documentation that the staff was ever trained in CPR or first aid.

checklists.contact_person: Licensor(s) Conducting this Inspection: McCabe Peterson OL Staff Observing Inspection:

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to provide direct supervision of clients during sleeping hours. During an on-site inspection, multiple interviews disclosed that all direct care staff were asleep while two clients ran away from the campsite in the middle of the night. The staff were unaware the clients were missing until they woke up the following morning.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule on 2 separate occasions.
The provider was out of compliance with this rule:

1. By failing to defer to the least restrictive method of behavior management available to control a situation. During on-site inspections, multiple interviews supported that several staff did not follow the behavior management system they have been trained in and conducted physical restraints on clients that were not an immediate harm to self or others.
2. By conducting physical restraints that caused clients undue physical discomfort and pain. During on-site inspections, multiple interviews supported that clients who have been placed in physical restraints are immediately and aggressively taken to the ground in a prone position and some staff use unnecessary pain compliance techniques. This is a repeat citation

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to submit critical incident reports to the Office. During an on-site inspection, the licensor found 2 separate incidents of staff members that were noncompliant with the provider code of conduct and client rights that the provider failed to report to the Office within one business day as required by rule. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff requiring clients to hike in temperatures above 90 degrees. During an on-site inspection, the licensing investigator reviewed a staff’s file. A record in this file, signed by the staff involved and one of the provider’s administrative team members, documented that the direct care staff made clients hike in temperatures in excess of 90 degrees fahrenheit.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule on 3 separate occasions.
The provider was out of compliance with this rule:
By neglecting to provide necessary health care by withholding prescription medication from a client. During an on-site inspection, the licensing investigator reviewed a client’s file. An incident report indicated a client’s medication was withheld for returning to the campsite from a hike later than the designated medication dispensing time.
By compromising the health and safety of clients when a staff failed to act and deter clients from acting. During an on-site inspection, provider documentation substantiated that a staff member was aware clients were going to run from the campsite. The staff failed to take safety precautions to prevent this incident from happening and instead laughed at the clients.
By a staff verbally mistreating clients. During onsite inspections, multiple interviews and provider documentation substantiated that a staff yelled at clients, called clients insulting names, and made threatening statements to clients.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff failing to follow the provider’s policy and procedure and client rights. During an on-site inspection, a staff file document was observed and recorded that the staff took naps during the day and failed to consistently supervise students in groups or out of sight.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by staff using an unnecessary pain compliance restraint technique. On June 6, 2023, during an onsite compliant investigation multiple interviews disclosed that a staff member used a pain compliance technique and acted outside of the providers policy and procedure and Utah Administrative Rule. The staff member initiated a pain compliance technique to a client that was not an immediate danger to themselves or others. The client was being passively resistive. The restraint resulted in undue physical discomfort and pain to the client. This was a repeated rule of noncompliance.

name: Three Peaks Ascent
address: 21185 N Pine Valley Rd, Beryl, UT, 84714

name: Turning Point Family Care, Inc. - Residential Treatment

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to submit over 10 critical incident reports to the Office of Licensing. During on and off-site inspections, the Office of Licensing conducted multiple client and staff interviews and reviewed documentation that supported noncompliance with this rule.

name: UHS Of Provo Canyon dba Provo Campus

findings.finding_text: The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-6(1)(a) by failing to protect a client from potential harm or acts of violence.  On  2/18/2026, a staff member punched a client.  The client was injured  and got stitches in their mouth.

name: UHS Of Provo Canyon dba Springville Campus

findings.finding_text: The licensee was out of compliance with R380-80-4(1) by failing to protect a client from mistreatment.  During the inspection the licensor reviewed video footage that showed a staff member physically pushing a client.

The provider is out of compliance with this rule by staff failing to keep a client free from harm or acts of violence. During an investigation inspection and review of video footage from 12/01/2024, it was observed that a staff member struck a client during a restraint. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider is out of compliance with rule by staff failing to keep clients safe from potential harm. While on site 11/26/2024, the investigator observed through video footage that the staff initiated a restraint that did not fall under the Handle With Care training.

The provider is out of compliance with rule by staff failing to keep clients free from harm or acts of violence. While on site, the licensor reviewed video footage of the critical incident. It was observed by the licensor that one staff involved in this incident had acted in a way where clients were harmed.

name: Utah Health Operations LLC dba Newport Academy
address: 251 W Weber Canyon Rd, Oakley, UT, 84055

findings.finding_text: The provider is non-compliant with this rule by failing to provide 15 minute line of sight check-ins with students. Prior to and during hygiene, a student went AWOL. This was unnoticed by staff members. Two staff members each believed the student was with the other staff member. Staff members took the word of other students and did not confirm where the missing student was. No line of sight check was done on the client who was missing for approximately 40 minutes before staff members realized the student was gone. Staff members were written up and re-trained due to their lack of action.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a critical incident by the next business day.  The incident occurred on 3/2/2024 and was reported on 3/5/2024.
This is a repeat noncompliance.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not submitting the critical incident report within one business day. Incident occurred on 10/26/2023 and was reported on 10/28/2023.

findings.rule_description: Direct supervision only by direct care staff
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not ensuring active supervision of all clients at all times. During an investigation inspection on 09/28/23, video footage documented two clients left unsupervised after a therapy group for at least 17 minutes on 09/20/2023.

findings.rule_description: Direct supervision only by direct care staff
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not ensuring active supervision of all clients at all times. On 09/10/2023, 3 clients were left unsupervised in their bedrooms for at least 54 minutes.

findings.rule_description: medication and hazardous items maintenance and storage
findings.finding_text: The provider is out of compliance with this rule by having a hygiene bag containing spray cologne out in one of the student bathrooms. During the walk through inspection, the licensor observed a hygiene bag in a student's bathroom with spray cologne in it.  This was locked up in the hygiene closet during the inspection. This is a repeat citation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by staff members not conducting line-of-sight checks every 15-minutes on clients. During the investigation inspection, provider documentation was reviewed that recorded multiple periods of time throughout the nights of 06/07/2024 and 06/08/2024 when line-of-sight checks exceeded 35 minutes.
This is a repeat citation.

findings.rule_description: Submitting application for an initial background screening
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to provide direct supervision for a staff member without an eligible or conditional background clearance determination. During the investigation inspection, video coverage was observed that recorded a staff member without an eligible or conditional background clearance working with clients independently.

findings.rule_description: Comply with all laws and rules
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to comply with state law. During an investigation inspection, the information collected substantiated that the provider failed to comply with Utah Code 26B-2-123(3)(b) and administered an involuntary chemical restraint on a client in a congregate care setting without the authorization of the Office of Licensing.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to provide supervision with line-of-sight check-ins no less frequently than every 15 minutes. During the investigation inspection, interviews and provider documentation verified that a client was left unsupervised in their bedroom for approximately 2 hours.

findings.rule_description: Provider shall protect clients from abuse, prevent abuse
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with the rule by failing to protect clients from alleged abuse by omitting information to an allied investigative agency. During the investigation inspection, provider documentation substantiated that information of an alleged sexual misconduct between 2 clients was not reported to CPS (Child Protective Services) in its entirety leading to the CPS referral to be unaccepted. When the omitted information was reported by the Office of Licensing, the CPS referral was accepted and the case was opened for investigation.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff member not ensuring the client(s) were free from potential harm. During an inspection, multiple interviews disclosed that the staff member was not providing active supervision when two clients engaged in inappropriate sexual misconduct on two separate occasions.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff member not providing supervision that is commensurate with the behaviors and needs of the clients involved in the incident.  During an inspection, multiple interviews disclosed that the staff member was not providing active supervision when two clients engaged in inappropriate sexual misconduct on two separate occasions. Multiple interviews disclosed that one of the clients involved in both incidents has a history of sexually acting out against his peers.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not maintaining the building free from hazards. During the onsite inspection, this licensor observed in two different client bedrooms that there was a hole in the wall.

findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not conducting physical 15-minute check-ins with clients. During the inspection a client was able to leave the facility without staffing being aware. This is a repeat noncompliance.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing supervision based off of clients behaviors. During the inspection clients were not supervised based of behaviors that they had previously demonstrated. This is a repeat noncompliance.

findings.rule_description: Services and supervision to meet each clients needs
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not providing supervision as required by a client's behavior. During the inspection a client with a history of sexually acting out was able to sexually act out with a peer. This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not reporting a critical incident to the Office within one business day. During review of critical incidents it was identified that the incident occurred on 06/29/2024 and was not reported until 07/02/2024.
This is a repeat noncompliance.

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by not removing all glass pieces from a  broken window. During the inspection the licensor saw a  broken window facing the outdoor basketball court that had small unattached glass particles around the window..

The provider was out of compliance with this rule by a staff failing to provide supervision commensurate with clients behaviors and needs on multiple occasions. During an on-site investigation inspection, multiple interviews and provider documentation substantiated the following incidents of noncompliance.
1. A staff member left their assigned post and area leaving clients without supervision. During this time, two clients engaged in sexual activity, and 
2. A staff member failed to supervise clients and was focused on a cell phone. During this time, clients left the staff’s supervision and engaged in sexual activity.
Upon discovery of these incidents, the provider took prompt action to address the noncompliances.

The provider was out of compliance by a staff failing to remain awake and appropriately supervise clients to ensure health and safety. During an on-site investigation inspection, multiple interviews disclosed that a staff member intentionally takes naps on the couch during their assigned shifts.

findings.rule_description: Licensed Program Complaints and Critical Incidents
findings.finding_text: The provider was out of compliance with this rule by failing to report a change to the clients’ environment that compromised their immediate health and safety. checklists.contact_person: Licensor(s) Conducting this Inspection: OL Staff Observing Inspection:
